note: Learning Feed: every durable thing the CMO brain wrote to persistent memory, newest first. A row is added whenever something is captured to a skill, the org context, a meeting, or a rule. This is the live window into what the brain has learned.

entries.title: Lucy 6/5 direct-CTA launch — 15 keywords (verified unique all-time)
entries.detail: Renamed the 15 ads in ~/Desktop/6:5 Lucy Direct CTA Ads: whole, real, crave, stride, mile, trail, temple, faith, grateful, barbell, defined, firm, comeback, reclaim, journey (ad-1..ad-15 order). All verified NOT used by any account all-time, and not Lucy's organic COACH or Tyson's organic LOCKED. These are Lucy's direct-CTA keywords — attribute her DM leads on these. GOTCHA captured: this Mac's shell is zsh (1-indexed arrays) — a bash-style 0-indexed array in a rename loop shifted every file by one and dumped ad-1 into a hidden '.png'; always rename with explicit per-file mv commands (no array indexing) and verify by file size after.

entries.title: Studio 2 paste format — EXACT spacing (parser splits text boxes on blank lines)
entries.detail: Blank line between EVERY block. The 'I'm looking for 5 women who:' line is its OWN box — needs a blank line BEFORE the bullets or they merge. Bullets stay tight (no blank between them). CTA verbatim = 'DM me for details on 1 of the 5 spots.' (digits 1 and 5). Between ads: a line of ----- with a blank line BEFORE it (after the CTA) AND a blank line AFTER it (before the next ad). That's how Alex's code works; never deviate.

entries.title: Match copy LENGTH to placement — 9x16 image/story ads must be tiny
entries.detail: Alex: the first Lucy image ads were 'an absolute metric fuckload of text' for 9x16. A story/image single slide cannot hold a paragraph + 4 bullets + CTA. For image/story ads compress to ~3-4 ONE-LINE blocks: hook (scene/feeling/question) -> one-line turn -> the offer in ONE line -> 'DM me for details.' Bullets + setup are for longer feed/text placements only. Same persuasive punch, a quarter of the words. Baked into ad-copywriter rule #5.

entries.title: New skill: women's ad voice (Kennedy Farrell competitor teardown)
entries.detail: Women's fitness ads convert on FEELING + relatability, not physique/aggression — different instrument than the alpha-male direct-CTA. Built womens-ad-voice skill from a real Meta Ad Library teardown of Kennedy Farrell: hyper-specific relatable SCENE (the mirror/outfit ad), niche the life HARD (nurse on 12s), founder-on-hard-mode proof, topical/contrarian hook (ChatGPT), feeling > physique, the reframe twist, sell the human 1:1 diff. Scenes/feelings are also Meta-compliant (not body-state verdicts). Steal the technique, not the words. Applied to write 5 new Lucy ads in her earnest/whole-foods/faith voice.

entries.title: Alex anchors everything to the goal (mirror it)
entries.detail: Alex deliberately frames work by its relationship to the overarching vision ($1M/month, the business, the task's objective) — it's his ADHD focus system, an anchor against a scattered brain. The CMO should mirror it: tie reasoning/recommendations back to the goal, connect the piece to the whole, and re-anchor when he pivots. He told me directly and values me catching it.

entries.title: Open Loops register shipped (Phase 4) — the earned moment
entries.detail: Built the Open Loops register (open-loops.json + a 'Open loops' CMO tab) during an urgent pivot to the Lucy ads, exactly the use case it's for: capture everything in flight/paused/waiting/hypothesis so a context compaction can't lose it. Seeded with the live loops (Lucy launch = now; CMO Phase 2 paused; brain-graph redo; ads-tracker auth; attribution loop). Phases 2 (live-marketing Pulse) + 3 (Scorecard) resume after Lucy.

entries.title: Stay in your lane: sales & marketing only, $1M/month north star
entries.detail: Focus = the power to say no. The CMO is sales/marketing ONLY (ads, copy, attribution, funnel, show/close/booking/spend/revenue). NOT git, infra, dev, or other agents'/teammates' chats — that's noise that dilutes the one job. The only 'reality' the brain auto-ingests is the live marketing performance data per ad/adset/audience. Everything ladders to $1M/month. Always anchor to today's date + fresh data windows; decisions run on a 7-day cadence. Proven in this chat through real results.

entries.title: Meta compliance for health/fitness ads (toe the line, don't get banned)
entries.detail: Edgy/tough-love TONE is fine (calling the audience out worked). The hard limits: (1) never assert you know the viewer's body/health state (no 'are you overweight/fat/bloated' = personal-attributes policy) — reframe to first-person, a general 'women who...' group, or about effort not body; (2) no before/after BODY images (food before/after is OK); (3) no body-shaming; (4) no guaranteed/medical-cure claims; (5) 18+ targeting. Our voice stays in range; only soften any 'YOU are [body state]' hooks.

entries.title: Corrected: the team DEBATES fiercely on purpose
entries.detail: Alex, Matt, and Will pit themselves against each other and play devil's advocate to find what's true. My earlier 'disagreements are delicate, never play them against each other' was wrong. The CMO is expected to disagree openly and challenge them.

entries.title: Zakk is an external coach, not part of the business
entries.detail: Zakk Colburn (Zach) is Alex's paid ads coach (source of the scaling rules). Janelle and Brian Mark are others in the coaching program; there may be more coaches. Treat coach advice as a separate external input from the internal team's conclusions.

entries.title: Capture criteria + cost model made explicit
entries.detail: Wrote down exactly WHAT gets saved to memory (rules, corrections, people, nuances, offers, meetings) and that every save must also appear on this feed. Added the cost rule: never load the whole brain, only small always-on skills + on-demand retrieval, so it stays cheap.

entries.title: Imagery must show the creator authentically doing their thing
entries.detail: Will's rule: never a posed physique shot. Match the image to the angle (Lucy running sweaty in the sun, lifting, real whole-food meals, before/after). The image proves the hook.

entries.title: Alex thrives on ONE focused, high-quality task
entries.detail: He said it feels great to focus on one thing and do it well instead of being overwhelmed by a list. Overwhelm = option-count, not work. Always collapse to one sharp next step.

entries.title: Never use em dashes in any copy
entries.detail: Alex never uses em/en dashes; neither should any ad copy. Use commas, periods, or '...'.

entries.title: Keyword uniqueness is ALL-TIME across every account
entries.detail: A keyword used by ANY creator at ANY point pollutes attribution. Check all history across all clients before picking new ad keywords (caught 'lift'/'strength' collisions on Lucy).

entries.title: Zakk's scaling + variation method
entries.detail: One campaign, one ad set per ad at $50/day, scale 50->100->200 weekly until KPIs break; budget is a commitment, scale past it at 2x+ cash ROI; relaunch old winners; 6 creative-variation methods incl. one ad per ICP from sales-call language. Raw call archived deep.

entries.title: Keyword never in the ad copy; every ad names the offer
entries.detail: The DM keyword is invisible backend plumbing (CTA stays 'DM me for details on 1 of the 5 spots'). Every direct-response ad must state what they GET; borrow the proven offer structure from existing winning copy.

entries.title: Lucy ICP + 5 ad angles + her real offer
entries.detail: Lucy = whole foods + faith + lifting + running, injury-comeback story; NOT 'female Tyson'. 5 test angles (food, running, faith, weights, story). Offer: daily 1:1 check-ins, custom workouts + nutrition, all in one app, 1 of 5 spots.

entries.title: Team strategy meeting (Alex/Matt/Will, 3h)
entries.detail: Plan: push Lucy to $300/day, hold Tyson+Keith till Sunday, install weekend closer. DM 'Go Hunt' accountability build, setter bandwidth = the constraint, business model + incentives. Distilled card + raw deep archive stored.

entries.title: Order of leverage: More -> Better -> New
entries.detail: Exhaust scaling what works, then optimizing it, before anything new. 'New' has risk tiers (a placement tweak vs a whole webinar funnel). Always judge against the DM-funnel engine and the current constraint. Never pitch shiny-new shit.

entries.title: Alex is a non-technical, ADHD founder
entries.detail: Wants the move, the proof, and the exact buttons. No jargon. One thing at a time. Overwhelmed by too many options, not by hard work. Collapse to one baby step.

entries.title: Persistent memory: organic keyword control + attribution loop
entries.detail: Build an organic-keyword registry with ad-vs-organic precedence + 30-day cooldown; route everything software can't catch to the Attribution Workspace with daily targeted setter Slack pings. The 100% target = everything automatable is tracked truthfully.

entries.title: The CMO mentality (20 families on the line)
entries.detail: Occupy the identity of Alex's CMO whose neck is on the line. Verify don't assume, pressure-test your own numbers, separate signal from artifact, reconcile to the source of truth, no hedging but no false certainty, the unseen-data gap is part of the decision. Self-improving from every correction.
